forecasts.reasoning: Border closure by Benin on May 6-7, 2025 involves serious dual issues: terrorism concerns (Mahmuda group + arms smuggling) and trade policy (soya/cashew export restrictions). While Nigerian House of Representatives took urgent action on May 13-14 urging diplomatic resolution, historical precedent shows Nigeria-Benin border closures last months/years, not weeks. The closure was ordered by President Talon himself and no diplomatic progress has been reported as of May 14. Combined with only a 55-day window until July 1 resolution date and no evidence of active negotiations, the likelihood of reopening within this timeframe is low.

Extremely high confidence based on explicit, recent commitment. Malta's Prime Minister Robert Abela announced on May 25, 2025 (just 6 days before knowledge cutoff) that Malta WILL formally recognize the State of Palestine on June 20, 2025, following the UN conference. Multiple credible sources confirm this definitive commitment. Since the question only requires one UN member country and Malta is a UN member state making an explicit public commitment with a specific June date, the probability is very high. The 0.12 uncertainty accounts only for remote possibilities of unforeseen diplomatic reversals or conference postponement.
